activated_paths.prediction: Single-measure construal should provide contextual warrant for controller identification and agreement feature alignment.
A recognized measure override should feed agreement alignment and licensing even with a formally plural head.
Plural-set construal should provide contextual warrant for controller identification and alignment in `none` examples.
Formal-style pressure should affect condemnation and attribution without deciding controller alignment.
Higher licensing for non-count singular construal should lower repair pressure for singular agreement.

prediction_tests.prediction: The graph should predict that a formally plural measure NP can license singular agreement when construed as a single measure, without treating the form as an attraction error.
prediction_tests.pass_condition: The held-out source supports singular agreement for plural measure NPs under single-measure construal and describes the pattern as an override or respecification, not as proximity error.
prediction_tests.fail_condition: The graph fails if the measure case requires a construct absent from the graph, or if the source treats the singular agreement as mere error, processing attraction, or raw preference.
prediction_tests.evidence_note: Checked against `cgel-subject-verb-agreement`, especially the further overrides section on plural measure nominals respecified as singular.

prediction_tests.prediction: The graph should predict that fused determiner-head agreement can vary with quantified-set construal while keeping formal-style avoidance separate from licensing.
prediction_tests.pass_condition: The source supports singular agreement for non-count construal, singular/plural alternatives for plural-set `none`, and formal-style avoidance for some plural variants without reducing all of this to processing attraction.
prediction_tests.fail_condition: The graph fails if fused determiner-head agreement requires a category/function construct for the agreement contrast itself, or if formal-style pressure must be treated as licensing failure.
prediction_tests.evidence_note: Checked against `cgel-subject-verb-agreement`, especially the fused determiner-head discussion of any, none, either, and neither.

cards.expected_survival_pattern: The graph should generalize from built-on agreement override cards to a held-out measure-NP singular override by routing the case through notional basis, controller identification, override pattern, and feature alignment.
cards.notes: Held-out survival. This supports the narrow agreement-controller graph as a real module, but no numeric score movement follows.

axes.community: Standard English speakers judging plural measure NPs under single-measure construal.
axes.task_framing: Measure construal and agreement value separated.

axes.community: Standard English speakers judging measure NP plus singular predicative complement.
axes.task_framing: Obligatory singular override and repair pressure separated.

cards.expected_survival_pattern: The graph should route fused determiner-head agreement through notional basis, controller identification, feature alignment, and task/ideology channels without adding a fused-head-specific graph.
cards.notes: Held-out survival. The graph covers the agreement contrast; category analysis may still belong to CAT, but it is not needed for the scoped agreement path.

axes.community: Standard English speakers judging fused determiner-heads with non-count singular construal.
axes.task_framing: Non-count singular construal and agreement value separated.

axes.community: Standard English speakers judging `none` over a plural set with plural agreement.
axes.task_framing: Plural-set construal and agreement value separated.

axes.community: Standard English speakers judging `none` over a plural set with singular agreement.
axes.task_framing: Singular agreement variant and plural-set construal separated.

axes.community: Standard English speakers judging fused determiner-head plural variants under formal-style framing.
axes.task_framing: Formal-style avoidance separated from licensing failure.
